mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in FEDFUNDS signals monetary tightening which increases discount rates and lowers equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year Rank IC is positive and greater than 0.03, or sign of return predictions is consistently opposite to the stated direction.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.5% after a recession, it indicates the economy is approaching full employment; further declines will generate wage inflation that pressures corporate margins and reduces equity returns over the forward quarter.
failure_condition: In at least 60% of triggered instances, the S&P 500 forward 60-day return is positive, or the spread between positive and negative returns is not statistically significant (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising FEDFUNDS and declining HOUST signals that tighter monetary policy is actively slowing the housing sector, a leading indicator of economic weakness that typically precedes negative equity returns over the next quarter.
regime_hint: any regime where monetary policy is active
failure_condition: The average forward 60-day return when FEDFUNDS increases and HOUST decreases is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), or the rank IC of the interaction signal is below 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime (CPIAUCSL year-over-year > 3%), a rise in INDPRO signals that supply is catching up to demand, reducing inflationary pressure and fostering a positive equity market response; in low inflation regimes the effect is muted.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY > 3%, the correlation between INDPRO changes and forward 20-day returns is negative or not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth (month-over-month increase in RSAFS) signals robust consumer spending, which drives corporate revenue and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns within the next week as markets reprice growth expectations.
failure_condition: The Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS month-over-month change and forward 5-day returns is ≤ 0 over a 2-year rolling window, or IC is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production index (INDPRO) falls below the 100 baseline level during an expansion, it signals a manufacturing downturn that will drag on broader economic activity and corporate profits, predicting negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of triggered events, the forward 60-day S&P 500 return is positive, or the average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment (PAYEMS) is declining but retail sales (RSAFS) are rising, it indicates a divergence: consumption is being supported by savings or credit rather than income, which is unsustainable and predicts negative equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle or post-crisis consumption without job growth
failure_condition: The average forward 20-day return during periods of declining PAYEMS and rising RSAFS is not negative, or the signal occurs in fewer than 30 instances over the testing period, making inference noisy.

mechanism_narrative: In a high unemployment regime (UNRATE above 5.5%), a rise in FEDFUNDS is interpreted as a vote of confidence by the central bank in the recovery, so equity markets react positively over the following quarter. In normal unemployment regimes, a rise in rates is contractionary and negative.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 5.5%, the correlation between FEDFUNDS changes and forward 60-day returns is negative or within zero's confidence interval, contradicting the hypothesized positive sign.
